description: This figure illustrates the complex three-dimensional morphologies of κ-phase precipitation in a 2Mn-8Al-0.2C (wt.%) low-density steel using complementary microscopy techniques. Panel (a) is an optical micrograph (scale bar 20 μm) showing a non-lamellar eutectoid microstructure after continuous cooling from 1400°C at 10°C/s and quenching at 870°C, where the κ-phase precipitates at the interfaces between austenite (γ) and ferrite (α) matrix. Panel (b) presents a scanning electron microscopy (SEM) image after deep etching (magnification 1.46 KX, scale bar 10 μm), revealing that the κ-phase grows preferentially along the long axis of austenite, forming blade-like structures. Panel (c) shows an SEM micrograph (magnification 2.00 KX, scale bar 10 μm) after isothermal treatment at 870°C for 15 minutes, demonstrating the complete decomposition of austenite and the formation of a distinctive cocoon-like κ-phase encapsulation structure. These images collectively capture the evolution of κ-phase from interface precipitation to fully enveloping the austenite, supporting the understanding of phase transformation kinetics in high-aluminum lightweight steels.
